title: epiverse
text: R metapackage maintained Economic Policy Institute (EPI). Similar tidyverse fastverse, provides single entry point install load collection EPI R packages.

title: epiverse
text: Distributed via r-universe GitHub (CRAN) sub-packages also r-universe GitHub Package sources hosted https://github.com/economic/ (EPI GitHub organization)

title: epiverse
code: # From r-universe install.packages("epiverse", repos = "https://economic.r-universe.dev")  # Or from GitHub remotes::install_github("economic/epiverse")  library(epiverse)

title: epiverse
text: Follows tidyverse metapackage pattern: - sub-packages listed Imports (Depends) metapackage controls attachment - 6 packages core — attached library(epiverse) - .onAttach hook attaches packages displays startup message versions - Conflict detection reporting - Utility functions: epiverse_packages(), epiverse_update(), epiverse_sitrep() - options(epiverse.quiet = TRUE) suppress startup messages - Dummy function referencing one symbol per import avoid R CMD check notes - MIT license - known conflicts included packages - Design document: plans/2026-03-14-epiverse-metapackage-design.md

title: Easily Install and Load the EPI R Package Suite
text: epiverse simplifies installing loading collection R packages maintained Economic Policy Institute. single call library(epiverse) attaches included packages reports function conflicts.

title: Easily Install and Load the EPI R Package Suite
code: library(epiverse) #> Warning: package 'epiverse' was built under R version 4.5.3 #> Warning: package 'epiextractr' was built under R version 4.5.3 #> Warning: package 'prefab' was built under R version 4.5.3 #> ── Attaching epiverse packages ─────────────────────────────── epiverse 0.1.0 ── #> ✔ epidatatools 1.0.1         ✔ prefab       0.1.0     #> ✔ epiextractr  0.11.1        ✔ realtalk     2026.3.11 #> ✔ epitargets   0.2.0         ✔ swadlr       0.2.0

title: Easily Install and Load the EPI R Package Suite
text: Install epiverse r-universe: add EPI r-universe repo .Rprofile can simply
code: install.packages(   "epiverse",   repos = c("https://economic.r-universe.dev", getOption("repos")) ) # Example .Rprofile options(repos = c(   epi = "https://economic.r-universe.dev",   getOption("repos") )) install.packages("epiverse")
